topic: NP pre-1990 linear drift — Std Model Explains; new verdict-boundary-note paragraph appended
change_summary: detail_verdict_length 1596→2184 (+36.8%), tldr_verdict_length 151→257 (+70.2%); sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-16T01:10:33Z; obe_skipped=6 (WIN-052/054/055/033/057/063 already re-reviewed since audit). The detail_verdict grew because a 'Verdict boundary note' paragraph was appended distinguishing WIN-006's Std Model Explains verdict from WIN-007/WIN-022's Misleading verdict; the tldr_verdict grew because the c4 hole was patched with the proposed wording. The c4 hole on tldr inconsistency is closed.
our_argument_summary: WIN-006 (Std Model Explains, NP pre-1990 linear drift): non-discriminating — both core-mantle convection and the dome's aetheric vortex can be drawn through the same NOAA NP.xy linear trend, and the dome's specific claimed rate (0.0466 deg/yr) cannot be reproduced from NP.xy by any standard metric. Additionally fails post-hoc test (indexed public dataset, no independent pre-registration, monitor hardcodes target on same commit as fetch line). New verdict boundary note paragraph distinguishes WIN-006 from WIN-007/WIN-022 (both Misleading) on the grounds that the pre-1990 linear drift is described 'at face value' with 'no false causal claim,' whereas WIN-007/WIN-022 introduce specific causal framings ('aetheric threshold crossing', 'phase transition') for the post-1990 acceleration.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (WIN list, line 68); raw-text/03-predictions.txt (PROS-002 parent prediction, lines 19-22); raw-text/08-live-power.txt (live monitor dashboard); sections.json part2b cross-validation
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome's source text gives bare numerical claim: '0.0466 deg/year linear drift from 1590-1990' attributed to 'NOAA NP.xy dataset.' Parent prediction PROS-002 frames the mechanism as 'aetheric vortex below threshold → slow linear precession.' No derivation from dome geometry (disc_radius, firmament_height) appears in either the WIN entry or the parent prediction — the 0.0466 rate is asserted to match observation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Per sections.json part2b text, WIN-006 is one of the WINs that 'fetch live data' (from NOAA NP.xy). The monitor.py source itself is not present in raw-text/ in this clone, but the longstanding tag (live_fetch) is consistent with the dome's own source claim and with prior reviews. Our detail_verdict text explicitly asserts that 'the monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line' — i.e., the target value (0.0466) is hardcoded even while the data IS fetched. The 'live_fetch' tag reflects that data is fetched; the post-hoc paragraph captures that the comparison threshold is hardcoded.
data_sources_used: NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position dataset (gufm1 pre-1900 blend + IGRF modern era; IGRF-14 current generation)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: PROS-002 frames the pre-1990 drift as 'below threshold' behavior of an unspecified 'aetheric vortex' — the threshold itself is not numerically pinned, so any observed change in slope can be assigned to either side of the threshold post-hoc.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new; the 'monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line' claim in detail_verdict_text is not directly verifiable in this clone (raw-text/ does not contain monitor.py), but matches the c4 framing accepted in prior cycles.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: sections.json part2b explicitly lists WIN-006 (with WIN-007) among 'WINs that fetch live data... from NOAA NP.xy.' Dome's WIN-006 row in raw-text/02-wins.txt cites 'Source: NOAA NP.xy dataset' rather than a static value. Prior cycle tag retained; no contrary evidence in this clone.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard geophysics (core-mantle convection / dynamo modeling, e.g., Livermore 2020 flux lobe dynamics) already describes the slow pre-1990 NMP drift. The dome's 'aetheric vortex precession below threshold' (PROS-002 raw text line 21) renames the same observed trajectory without producing a different numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: NOAA NGDC NP.xy is an indexed public dataset (gufm1 reconstructions of pre-1900 dates have been published since the early 2000s; IGRF series since the 1960s, with IGRF-14 the current generation). No independent pre-registration of '0.0466 deg/yr' in any timestamping venue. Per our own detail_verdict_text, monitor commit ordering does not satisfy the dome author's own 'timestamped before confirming data was pulled' standard.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 0.0466 deg/yr value is not derived from any dome-specific geometric parameter (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, sun_altitude, moon_altitude). PROS-002 invokes 'aetheric vortex' but provides no formula. Our review's quantitative cross-check (interpreting 0.0466 deg/yr as great-circle arc ≈ 5.2 km/yr, well below the 10-15 km/yr observed pre-1990 rate; or as longitude at 78N ≈ 1.1 km/yr, even further off) supports the assertion that the value 'cannot be reproduced from NP.xy by any standard metric.'

holes_found.description: The new Verdict boundary note rests on a distinction that doesn't survive a one-step trace to the parent prediction. It claims WIN-006 has 'no false causal claim, only an absence of discriminating power' while WIN-007/WIN-022 are Misleading because they 'introduce specific causal framings... for the post-1990 acceleration.' But the dome's own PROS-002 parent prediction (raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 19-22) explicitly invokes 'aetheric vortex precession below threshold' as the mechanism for the pre-1990 linear drift — the very thing the note says WIN-006 lacks. WIN-006 and WIN-007 are two halves of the same parent prediction; both inherit 'aetheric vortex' as the named mechanism. A dome defender will read the boundary note as: 'when the dome's prediction fits the observed shape, we excuse the mechanism label; when it overreaches we cite the mechanism label as a category error.' That looks like an empirically-conditioned verdict, not a methodological distinction.
holes_found.recommendation: Either: (a) Rewrite the boundary note to acknowledge that WIN-006 inherits 'aetheric vortex' framing from PROS-002 and explain why the verdict still differs — the cleanest version is that WIN-006's row text is observation-focused (a fitted slope match) while WIN-007/WIN-022 row text foregrounds the borrowed-from-thermodynamics labels ('threshold crossing,' 'phase transition') that import false specificity (no order parameter, no critical exponent, no symmetry breaking) the dome cannot back up. The distinction is type-of-language inside the WIN, not absence-of-mechanism in the parent. OR: (b) Drop the binary distinction and reframe as 'these three WINs cluster on the same parent prediction; we apply Std Model Explains to the observational half (linear-fit shape) and Misleading to the framing-laden halves (acceleration with borrowed thermodynamic terminology).' Either fix is small (~2 sentences); the current note is the version most easily attacked.

stronger_arguments.description: The numerical reproducibility critique is the strongest move and could be foregrounded more cleanly. The current detail_verdict_text leads with 'non-discriminating' and only mentions 'cannot be reproduced from NP.xy by any standard metric' as a parenthetical. Inverting the order — lead with the numerical falsification, then add 'and even if the number were reproducible, the linear trend is non-discriminating' — makes the argument harder to dismiss. The current order lets a defender concede 'non-discriminating' (which can be reframed as 'consistent with both models' = pro-dome) without confronting the harder claim that 0.0466 deg/yr doesn't even match NOAA NP.xy.
sources: Quantitative verification this cycle: 0.0466 deg/yr ≈ 5.2 km/yr (great-circle) or ≈ 1.1 km/yr (longitude at 78°N) vs observed 10-15 km/yr pre-1990 / 15-18 km/yr 1975-1990 — factor-of-2-to-15 mismatch.

stronger_arguments.description: The duplicated post-hoc paragraph across WIN-006/007/022/036 (all NOAA NP.xy derivations) creates a TF-IDF dilution problem — each individual WIN repeats the same ~600 chars of post-hoc boilerplate. Consider hoisting it once into Part 3.1b and replacing per-WIN copies with a one-sentence reference. Improves signal-to-noise without weakening any individual argument.
sources: Cross-WIN scan this cycle: WIN-006, WIN-007, WIN-022, WIN-036 all contain near-identical 'Under the dome author's own definition... legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b' paragraph.

kernel_of_truth.description: The pre-1990 NMP drift IS roughly linear and slow — both the dome's PROS-002 (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:20) and standard NOAA NGDC reconstructions agree on this qualitative shape. The dome correctly identified a real, well-documented phenomenon and bet on the historical slope continuing through 1990 (data/wins.json:WIN-006.detail_evidence). That is a non-trivial fact about the data — many flat-Earth claims are wrong even at the descriptive level; this one is descriptively correct (data/wins.json:WIN-006.tldr_evidence).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being descriptively correct about a slope direction is necessary but not sufficient for confirmation under the dome's own predictive standard (raw-text/01-home.txt 'timestamped before confirming data was pulled,' echoed in data/wins.json:WIN-006.detail_verdict_text). Standard geophysics also describes the slope (data/wins.json:WIN-006.detail_evidence 'core-mantle boundary convection patterns'); the slope is non-discriminating. And the specific numerical claim — 0.0466 deg/yr — does not survive the reproducibility test against NP.xy (data/wins.json:WIN-006.tldr_verdict; cross-check this cycle: 0.0466 deg/yr ≈ 5.2 km/yr great-circle vs observed 10-15 km/yr in our own detail_evidence). The dome wins the qualitative description and loses the quantitative match.

deeper_analysis: The c4 hole on tldr_verdict inconsistency is now closed — current tldr_verdict matches verbatim the c4 recommendation, and detail/tldr are aligned on the '0.0466 cannot be reproduced from NP.xy' framing. The change that triggered re-review is the new 'Verdict boundary note' paragraph (~580 new chars in detail_verdict_text). The note's intent is good — it preempts the 'why is this Std Model Explains and not Misleading?' question that a careful reader would ask after comparing WIN-006 to WIN-007/WIN-022. The execution is brittle. The note draws a binary between 'face-value description' (WIN-006) and 'specific causal framing' (WIN-007/WIN-022), but the dome's parent prediction PROS-002 actually frames both halves causally ('aetheric vortex precession' below threshold, 'aetheric vortex threshold crossing' above). The real distinction is rhetorical specificity within the WIN row text — WIN-007 and WIN-022 import thermodynamics-specific vocabulary ('phase transition,' 'threshold crossing') with no underlying physics (no order parameter, no critical exponent, no symmetry breaking — WIN-022's detail_verdict_text actually says this), whereas WIN-006's row text is observation-statement only. A defender can collapse the note's binary by quoting PROS-002 back at us. The fix is small.

Parent prediction PROS-002 (raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 19-22) confirms both halves of the prediction (pre-1990 slow linear drift, post-1990 sharp phase transition) attribute to 'aetheric vortex' mechanism
citations_unchecked: NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position records — not directly fetched this cycle; relied on documented public availability as established in prior cycles
IGRF-14 current generation status — not directly verified this cycle; consistent with public IGRF release cadence (IGRF-13 released 2020, IGRF-14 expected 2025) per author's prior statements
Chulliat & Newitt 2010 (cited transitively via WIN-007) — not re-verified this cycle

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender accepting the review's six methodological principles (especially 'discriminatory power required') still has a usable opening here. They concede the linear drift is non-discriminating in isolation, but argue: '(1) WIN-006 is not a standalone claim — it is the lower-threshold half of PROS-002, whose other half is WIN-007. Your review classifies WIN-006 as Std Model Explains (non-discriminating) while classifying WIN-007 as Misleading (mechanism overreach). But both inherit the same parent mechanism: aetheric vortex precession below threshold, crossing above. Either both are non-discriminating descriptions of fitted slopes (the dome got lucky on the shape but not the rate, per your own quantitative critique) — in which case both should be Std Model Explains, and your Misleading-for-WIN-007 verdict is conditioned on the post-1990 acceleration overshooting predictions; or both are category-error mechanism claims, in which case WIN-006 should also be Misleading. Your binary verdict boundary at the threshold inflection point doesn't survive a 30-second trace to the parent prediction.' (2) On the numerical reproducibility critique itself, a defender can argue 0.0466 deg/yr is intended as the linear-regression slope over 1590-1990 with the centennially-varying NMP latitude factored in (a model parameter, not a raw great-circle arc), and your quick conversion to km/yr at a fixed latitude is a strawman of what the dome means. This concedes that the documentation is inadequate but argues the value is not as obviously refuted as the tldr_verdict suggests.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Address the defender's (1) directly in the Verdict boundary note as recommended in holes_found. For (2), tighten the 'cannot be reproduced by any standard metric' tldr language by naming what was checked (e.g., 'cannot be reproduced as great-circle arc, longitude rate, or latitude rate at any plausible NMP-latitude weighting'). The current 'any standard metric' is sweeping and a defender can call it under-specified. A targeted fix is to add a 2-3 word qualifier; not a structural change.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-006 (Std Model Explains, pre-1990 linear drift) and WIN-007 (Misleading, post-1990 acceleration) are derived from the same parent prediction PROS-002, which invokes 'aetheric vortex' as the mechanism for BOTH halves. The new Verdict boundary note in WIN-006.detail_verdict_text claims WIN-006 lacks 'false causal claim,' but this is contradicted by the parent prediction text the dome itself publishes. WIN-036 (Std Model Explains, NP deviation >18 deg) and WIN-007 (Misleading, same NOAA NP.xy data, also concerns the post-1990 trajectory) are also potentially in tension on the same dimension. WIN-022 explicitly self-identifies as a duplicate of WIN-007 in our review text (data/wins.json:WIN-022.finding 'Duplicate of WIN-007'), which is internally consistent but reinforces that we treat the WIN-006/007/022 cluster as separate verdicts on what is, in the dome's own framing, one parent prediction. This isn't a fatal inconsistency — the verdicts are defensible — but the boundary rationale needs to be sharpened.

claims_checked: Dome's 0.0466 deg/yr drift rate vs our review's 10-15 km/yr pre-1990 / 15-18 km/yr in 1975-1990 decade
Interpretive plausibility of 0.0466 deg/yr under multiple geometric conventions
quantitative_verification.computations: At NMP latitude ~78°N: (a) Great-circle arc interpretation: 0.0466 deg × 111 km/deg = 5.17 km/yr. (b) Longitude-only at 78°N: 0.0466 × 111 × cos(78°) = 1.08 km/yr. Our review's observed rate of 10-15 km/yr (pre-1990 broad) and 15-18 km/yr (1975-1990 decade) is 2-15× higher than any interpretation of 0.0466 deg/yr. The dome's '~0.05 deg/year for 400 years' (PROS-002) is also broadly consistent with 0.0466 deg/yr as a centuries-averaged rate, but our review's km/yr figures correspond to recent-decade rates leading up to 1990 (when the NMP was at lower latitude and drift was accelerating into the transition).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Not a discrepancy in our review per se — our 10-15 km/yr range and the dome's 0.0466 deg/yr are about different things (recent-decade observed rate vs centuries-averaged claimed rate), and our detail_evidence does qualify 'roughly' and explicitly notes acceleration in the 1975-1990 decade. The quantitative result REINFORCES our 'cannot be reproduced from NP.xy by any standard metric' claim — under any geometric convention, 0.0466 deg/yr underestimates the empirically observed drift rate in the decades preceding 1990 by a factor of 2 to 15. The opportunity (per stronger_arguments above) is to surface this calculation explicitly somewhere instead of leaving the 'no standard metric' assertion bare.

summary_for_decider: WIN-006 c5 verdict still holds (Std Model Explains is correct). The c4 hole on tldr_verdict synchronization is closed — current tldr matches the c4 recommendation. The new 'Verdict boundary note' paragraph patches a real reader-confusion problem (why is WIN-006 Std Model Explains while WIN-007/WIN-022 are Misleading?) but the rationale offered is fragile — a dome defender can collapse the binary by quoting parent prediction PROS-002 which invokes 'aetheric vortex' for both halves of the prediction. Recommend a small (~2-sentence) rewrite of the boundary note as a minor patch (suggested wording in holes_found). Also two non-blocking stronger_arguments: (a) re-order detail_verdict to lead with the quantitative falsification rather than the non-discriminating framing, (b) consider hoisting the duplicated post-hoc paragraph (now in WIN-006/007/022/036) into Part 3.1b. No verdict change. No carry-over from prior cycles.
